response: We can update your email on file. Please spell the full email address and we’ll replace the old one.
We can update your phone number on file. Please confirm the old number and the new number.
We can remove outdated phone numbers from your chart. Please confirm which number to delete.
I’m sorry about that. I can help with scheduling, prep instructions, results, billing, and general office questions. What’s the main thing you’re trying to do right now?
If you have urgent symptoms or significant concerns, we can try to move your follow-up sooner. Otherwise two weeks is standard for pathology availability.
Yes—schedule a follow-up (phone or in-office) to discuss any precancerous findings and confirm the surveillance interval and plan.
If liver enzymes/metabolic risk are being monitored, follow-up intervals like 6 months are common. We can schedule as recommended.
Follow-up can be by phone or in office. We typically schedule it about two weeks after the procedure.
After H. pylori treatment, follow-up testing is typically needed to confirm eradication (often breath test). We can schedule follow-up accordingly.
We usually schedule follow-up about two weeks after the procedure so pathology results are available.
Follow-ups are usually scheduled about 2 weeks after the procedure so we have the full report and pathology results.
Yes — we can email the PandaDocs intake forms. Please email shelbygimd@gmail.com with your full name and date of birth, and my supervisor will email you the forms and next steps.
Patients are asked to complete intake forms through PandaDocs, either in advance by email or in person at the office. Insurance cards and demographic information are also collected.
If warts/HPV lesions are suspected near the anal opening, Dr. Shelby typically refers to a colorectal surgeon for evaluation because some lesions can carry cancer risk.
You can email forms to our office and we’ll route them to Dr. Shelby for review. Some forms may require an appointment depending on the request.
For records requests, please provide patient name/DOB and the date range (e.g., 2022–present) and confirm the fax number. We can then process and fax records.
If a fax wasn’t received, please confirm the correct fax number and we will resend the report.
If results went to the wrong PCP, we can update your PCP and resend/fax your report. Please confirm the correct PCP name and fax number if needed.
We can fax records to your provider. Please provide patient name/DOB, date range, and the correct fax number.
We can send the full procedure + pathology report and follow-up recommendations to your provider by fax. Please confirm the correct fax number and date range.
If your office didn’t receive the fax, please confirm the correct fax number and we will resend the report.
We can send a PandaDoc reminder so the forms reappear in your inbox. Check spam/junk if you don’t see it.
I can re-send your PandaDoc forms. Please check your inbox and spam folder for an email from our office.
Your forms come via PandaDoc. Click “Open document” and complete them online—no printing needed. If you can’t find it, we can re-send the link.
